records.ety: [Gr. <?/; 'i`dios proper, peculiar + <?/, <?/, to suffer: cf. F. idiopathie.]

defs: A peculiar, or individual, characteristic or affection.
quotes: All men are so full of their own fancies and idiopathies, that they scarce have the civility to interchange any words with a stranger.

defs: A morbid state or condition not preceded or occasioned by any other disease; a primary disease.
